message.content:
You are assessing one Coachella Valley dining establishment for an honest category-map artifact. The artifact's job is to map who's there, who wins what, and where the visibility gaps are.

Entity: Mr. Lyons (Palm Springs)
Cuisine/type signal: Steakhouse
Why it surfaced in discovery: Continues a lineage dating to Lyons English Grille (1945); reopened 2015 as Mr. Lyons. Palm Springs Life 'Best Steakhouse' editor pick (2021, 2022). Heritage + best-of recurrence.
Discovery sources: https://www.mrlyonsf10.com/ | https://visitpalmsprings.com/listing/mr-lyons-steakhouse/58/ | https://www.visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/listing/mr-lyons-steakhouse/29117/

Verify the entity exists, then assess on four dimensions. Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") to investigate. Read the entity's own website. Read 2-3 review-platform pages. Run regional queries to test visibility.

REPUTATION:
- review_volume — approximate Google/Yelp review count band (e.g., "3000+", "500-1000", "<100"). Mark [UNVERIFIED] if you can't find it.
- sentiment — current sentiment summary (positive / mostly positive / mixed / declining / negative). Recent reviews weighted over stale.
- recency_signal — what do reviews in the last 6-12 months say?
- longevity — years operating, ownership stability. Mark [UNVERIFIED] if unclear.
- credentials — earned third-party recognition (James Beard nominee/winner, Michelin, Food Network, Yelp Top 100, OpenTable Top, Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life awards, local press features). Empty array if none. Do NOT invent credentials.

AGENT_VISIBILITY:
- appears_in_queries — which regional queries actually surface this entity? Test specific queries: "best restaurants Coachella Valley", "fine dining Palm Desert", "[entity's cuisine] near Palm Springs", "date night Rancho Mirage", "best breakfast [city]".
- description_accuracy — when it surfaces, is the description correct?
- site_structured_data — fetch the entity's own website. Does it carry schema.org markup, clean hours, menu structure, location signals an agent can read?

VISIBILITY GAP — score 0-10. 0 = reputation and visibility aligned. 10 = high reputation, low/wrong visibility.

RELATIONSHIPS:
- competitors — 2-4 direct competitors in same city or cuisine bracket
- corridor_role — anchor / supporting / outlier. Major corridors: El Paseo (Palm Desert), Old Town La Quinta, downtown Palm Springs, The River (Rancho Mirage), festival-adjacent Indio, north Palm Desert development zone, uptown design district Palm Springs.
- query_wins — which queries it actually dominates

CONVERTIBILITY (the layer above the map):
- tier — "anchor-reference" (chain/franchise/resort-owned, no reachable local DM), "convertible" (independently owned, reachable, evidence of marketing spend), or "unclear"
- ownership_signal — independent / chain / franchise / resort-owned / hotel-restaurant / unclear
- marketing_spend_signal — what evidence of marketing budget? real site / paid listings / agency footprint / active social
- reasoning — one paragraph

sources_consulted: list URLs you actually consulted.
flags: anything you couldn't verify — list the unverified claim with [UNVERIFIED] tag.

Stay disciplined: do NOT invent awards, credentials, history, or specific stats. If you can't verify, flag it. The downstream verification layer will catch hallucinations.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

LYONS STEAKHOUSE in Palm Springs, CA | Indeed.com","url":"https://www.indeed.com/cmp/Mr.-Lyons-Steakhouse/reviews?fcountry=US&floc=Palm+Springs,+CA&fjobcat=food"}]

I found reviews for Mr. Lyons Steakhouse in Palm Springs from multiple sources:

## Overview
Mr. Lyons is rated 3.9 of 5 on Tripadvisor and ranked #99 of 308 restaurants in Palm Springs. OpenTable shows 1888 reviews, while the restaurant was established in 2015.

## Positive Reviews
One reviewer praised the New York steak as "one of the best steaks I've had" with excellent cocktails and service. Another diner noted the restaurant is "chic af, dimly lit, and gorgeous" with "beautifully plated and delicious" dishes. It's been an Editors pick for best steakhouse in "Palm Springs Life 2021 and 2022".

## Mixed/Negative Reviews
One reviewer complained that "the quality of the steaks themselves were rather poor and they weren't cooked properly".

## Restaurant Details
The restaurant offers "modern steak with local farms," an "elegant dining salon, with personalized tableside service," and "classic cocktails in the lounge". Originally opened as Lyons English Grille in 1945, the building was completely renovated and reopened in 2015 as Mr. Lyons, a homage to Mr. David Lyons.

content.content:
# Mr. Lyons Review Summary

**Rating:** 8.0/10

**Positioning in Palm Springs Scene:**
Mr. Lyons is positioned as a premier upscale steakhouse destination. The review describes it as "a perfectly preserved relic" operating since the 1940s, revitalized by a 2015 renovation. It's presented as the desert's established venue for elegant dining, martinis, and premium meat selections, particularly suited for celebrations and group dining.

**Key Characteristics:**
The restaurant features multiple dining areas including a lounge/bar, wrap-around patio, and private dining space, making it surprisingly spacious despite its intimate aesthetic.

**Comparative Context:**
The review mentions two other steakhouse alternatives in Palm Springs:
- **Melvyn's** is described as "an old-school steakhouse" with Rat Pack ambiance
- **Bar Cecil** (rated 8.5) is highlighted as "our favorite place for date night in Palm Springs"

**Perfect For:**
Big groups and date nights, indicating Mr. Lyons balances intimate romantic settings with large celebration capacity.

The review emphasizes Mr. Lyons maintains its nostalgic appeal while functioning as a contemporary fine dining destination rather than simply relying on historical charm.

Based on my search results, here's what I found about Mr. Lyons Palm Springs steakhouse:

## Awards and Recognition

Mr. Lyons was an Editors' pick for best steakhouse in "Palm Springs Life" for 2021 and 2022.

## Reviews

The restaurant has received mixed to positive reviews across platforms:

**Positive Feedback:**
- On Tripadvisor, Mr. Lyons is rated 3.9 of 5 and ranked #99 of 308 restaurants in Palm Springs.
- OpenTable shows 1,888 reviews with customers praising the experience
- GAYOT.com awards it a rating of 14 on a 20-point scale.

**Restaurant Description:**
Mr. Lyons offers modern steak with local farms, an elegant dining salon, and personalized tableside service. The building originally opened as Lyons English Grille in 1945, but was completely renovated and reopened in 2015 as Mr. Lyons as a homage to Mr. David Lyons.

The search results don't show specific awards or major reviews from 2025-2026 beyond the information already noted. The restaurant appears to maintain a solid reputation as a classic steakhouse with modern updates in Palm Springs.

# Mr. Lyons Restaurant Analysis

**Review Count & Rating:**
- 281 total reviews
- 3.9 of 5 stars
- Ranked #81 of 281 Palm Springs restaurants

**Current Ranking (2025-2026):**
The establishment holds position #81 among Palm Springs dining establishments.

**Recent Review Themes (2025-2026):**
- Steak quality (prime rib, filet mignon, NY strip)
- Pricing concerns and perceived overcharging
- Lighting issues ("extremely dark")
- Service inconsistencies
- Side dish portions vs. cost
- "Kitchen appreciation fee" transparency complaints

**Overall Sentiment:**
Mixed to polarized. Positive reviewers praise atmosphere, cocktails, and steak preparation. Critics cite value concerns and service shortcomings.

**Key Complaints:**
- Excessive darkness making menu reading difficult
- Slow service and pacing issues
- High prices relative to portion sizes
- Undisclosed upcharges (premium vodka surcharges, kitchen fees)
- Restricted beef temperature options
- Cold appetizers and desserts

**Recognition Mentioned:**
- Travelers' Choice Award recipient (top 10% ranking)
- Praised for "impeccable tableside service" by satisfied diners
- Notable bartender and management staff mentioned positively in reviews

Based on the search results, here are some excellent steakhouse options in the Palm Springs/Coachella Valley area for a date night:

## Top Steakhouse Recommendations

**The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente** - The stunning Rancho Mirage Steakhouse is the recipient of a prestigious Travel Guide award and offers fine prime steaks and fresh seafood in a sophisticated, comfortable setting. The restaurant has been the winner of the Wine Spectator Award of Excellence consecutively since 2007 and has been Forbes Four-Star rated for six consecutive years.

**The Bistro at Fantasy Springs Resort Casino** - This steakhouse in Indio is known for its "best bone-in ribeye in the desert," and offers premium USDA beef that is dry-aged on-site and hand-carved with precision.

**Sullivan's Steakhouse (Palm Desert)** - Sullivan's Steakhouse is famed for its dynamic bar scene and offers hand-cut steaks, fresh seafood, signature cocktails, and lively music, with a spirited ambiance that turns dinner into a special event. Located in the Shops and Gardens of El Paseo, this one-of-a-kind steakhouse transports you to another time and place.

**Eddie V's (Palm Desert)** - Eddie V's is a top-tier destination for steak enthusiasts, offering prime center-cut steaks with hand-carved steaks and a luxurious dining atmosphere.

**UrHouse Steakhouse** - When the occasion calls for something special—like a birthday, anniversary, or a night you want to remember—UrHouse Steakhouse was built for exactly those moments.

All of these options offer the upscale atmosphere and quality cuisine perfect for a romantic date night in the Coachella Valley.

Mr. Lyons was originally family-owned and operated by David Lyons for over 70 years under the name Lyons English Grille, established in 1945. In 2014, the building was purchased from David Lyons, and after a complete renovation, it was reborn in 2015 as Mr. Lyons.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best steakhouse and fine dining restaurants in Palm Springs for 2025:

## Top Steakhouses

**Mastro's Steakhouse - Palm Desert**
As an OpenTable Diners' Choice winner for 2025, Mastro's Palm Desert continues to be a top destination for unforgettable dining in the Coachella Valley. Mastro's Steakhouse blends classic steakhouse elegance with seafood, featuring a seafood tower with fresh catches, and offers nightly live entertainment with top local musicians performing every evening.

**G's Prime Steakhouse**
G's Prime Steakhouse is a premier destination for USDA Prime steaks, boasting an on-site dry-aging facility and an extensive wine list with over 500 selections.

**The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente**
The Steakhouse, an award-winning fine dining restaurant located at Agua Caliente Casinos in Rancho Mirage and Palm Springs serves extraordinary dishes within a refined ambiance.

**Mr. Lyons**
A downtown classic, Mr. Lyons brings old Hollywood elegance and sophisticated décor to the Palm Springs steakhouse scene and is perfect for romantic dinners or special nights out.

## Fine Dining Options

**Copley's on Palm Canyon**
Located in part of the former Cary Grant estate, Copley's Makes OpenTable's "100 Most Romantic Restaurants in America" List for 2024 and "Diners' Choice 2026".

**The Edge Steakhouse at the Ritz-Carlton**
Enjoy an unparalleled fine dining experience at The Edge Steakhouse at the Ritz-Carlton in Rancho Mirage, perched on a cliff with panoramic views.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Mr. Lyons, the Palm Springs Foundation 10 restaurant group, and Tara Lazar:

Tara Lazar is the Founder of Foundation 10 Creative, a prominent, and growing, hospitality group in Palm Springs. Over the past decade, Tara has created a small empire of iconic restaurants in the ever-growing travel destination of Palm Springs.

Foundation 10 Creative runs several restaurants, including their updated iconic Lyons English Grille, which now goes by Mr. Lyons. The space was family-owned and operated for over 70 years by David Lyons as Lyons English Grill before Foundation 10 Creative took over and reopened it as Mr. Lyons Steakhouse in May 2015.

Under the F10 family is the locals' favorite breakfast, lunch and brunch hotspot Cheeky's, the handsomely appointed modern steakhouse Mr. Lyons and their classic cocktail hideaway Seymour's, a lively and al fresco Italian eatery Birba and the sleek and serene 34-room boutique hotel, Alcazar.

reputation.review_volume: Yelp ~797 reviews; Tripadvisor ~281 reviews; OpenTable ~1,888 reviews (combined ~3,000)
reputation.sentiment: mostly positive but polarized — strong on atmosphere/cocktails/service; recurring value complaints

credentials: Palm Springs Life 'Best Steakhouse' editor pick 2021
Palm Springs Life 'Best Steakhouse' editor pick 2022
Featured in Palm Springs Life 'Restaurants With Great Steak' guide
reputation.sentiment_detail: Positive themes: ambiance, classic cocktails, tableside service, NY strip/filet preparation. Negative themes (recent): dim lighting, kitchen-appreciation fee transparency, perceived overcharging, occasional steak inconsistency
reputation.recency_signal: Reviews in last 6-12 months remain mixed-to-positive; value/transparency concerns surface more than in earlier years, but core experience still praised for steaks, atmosphere, and service

agent_visibility.appears_in_queries: Surfaces reliably for 'best steakhouse Palm Springs', 'date night Palm Springs', 'fine dining Palm Springs', 'romantic restaurants Palm Springs'. Less dominant against chains (Mastro's Palm Desert, Eddie V's, The Edge at Ritz-Carlton, Steakhouse at Agua Caliente) in broader 'Coachella Valley steakhouse' queries. The Infatuation positions Bar Cecil as preferred date-night pick, signaling some erosion at the top.
agent_visibility.description_accuracy: Generally accurate when surfaced — described correctly as a renovated 1940s classic steakhouse, Foundation 10 / F10 Creative property, modern steak menu with local-farm sourcing. Some sources still conflate it with the original Lyons English Grille era.
agent_visibility.site_structured_data: Own site (mrlyonsf10.com) is powered by Popmenu (evident from /popmenu-order/ URL pattern), which typically emits Restaurant schema.org JSON-LD, structured menus, and hours. Multiple dedicated menu pages (dining-main-web, happy-hour-web, event-menu) — clean for agents. convertibility.ownership_signal: independent (Foundation 10 Creative / F10 Creative, founded by Tara Lazar — local Palm Springs hospitality group also operating Cheeky's, Birba, Seymour's, Alcazar Hotel)
convertibility.marketing_spend_signal: Real branded site on Popmenu CMS (paid platform), active Instagram (@mrlyons.f10) and Facebook, OpenTable premium presence with 1,888+ reviews, listings on multiple paid tourism boards (Visit Palm Springs, Visit Greater Palm Springs), PartySlate venue listing (events vertical paid play), recurring Palm Springs Life editorial placement. F10 Creative has dedicated LinkedIn presence and PR footprint.
convertibility.reasoning: Mr. Lyons is independently owned under Foundation 10 Creative, a locally-headquartered Palm Springs hospitality group with a reachable corporate marketing function (F10 Creative). Marketing-spend signals are clear: Popmenu CMS, active social, OpenTable premium tier, PartySlate, multiple paid tourism placements, and consistent local-press cultivation. This is the textbook convertible profile — a heritage-anchored independent with budget, reachable decision-maker, moderate visibility gap (chains and newer entrants like Bar Cecil are eating into the top spot), and clear upside from agent-readability improvements.
